factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with moderate growth in residential and commercial construction positively impacting HVAC businesses, while industrial equipment activity is slower.

evidence.text: Residential and Commercial new construction has seen moderate growth in the United States which is positively impacting the results of our Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) businesses. Non-residential new construction continues to grow slowly in Europe and Asia. In addition, HVAC equipment replacement and aftermarket continue to experience steady growth. However, we have seen slower worldwide industrial equipment activity, which is slightly offset by continued growth in services.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand trends with positive growth in residential and commercial construction offset by slower industrial activity.

factors.summary: The company is successfully improving pricing, with evidence of pricing improvements in excess of material inflation and productivity benefits.

evidence.text: Operating margin increased from 13.6% for the three months ended September 30, 2015 to 14.1% for the same period in 2016. The increase was primarily the result of pricing improvements in excess of material inflation (0.5%) and productivity benefits in excess of other inflation (0.2%).

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Pricing improvements exceed material inflation and productivity benefits, leading to margin expansion.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Climate segment, driven by service, parts, and replacement revenue streams.

evidence.text: Our growing geographic and industry diversity, coupled with our large installed product base, provides growth opportunities within our service, parts and replacement revenue streams. In addition, we are investing substantial resources to innovate and develop new products and services which we expect will drive our future growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Climate segment, driven by service and replacement revenue.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable with moderate increases in both Climate and Industrial segments, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency impacts.

evidence.text: Net revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2016 increased by 2.3% , or $80.9 million , compared with the same period in 2015 , which resulted from the following: Volume/product mix 2.1  % Acquisitions 0.2  % Pricing 0.3  % Currency translation (0.3 )% Total 2.3  %

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is stable with moderate increases in Climate and Industrial segments, partially offset by unfavorable foreign currency impacts.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing in the Climate segment, particularly in Commercial and Residential HVAC businesses, while Industrial segment volumes are flat due to market weakness.

evidence.text: Climate net revenues increased by 2.9% or $ 79.9 million , compared with the same period of 2015. The primary driver of the increase related to incremental volumes in both the Commercial HVAC and Residential HVAC businesses. Commercial HVAC results reflect continued improvements in service and parts while Residential HVAC results increased with strong order growth and improved pricing.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes growing in Climate segments but flat in Industrial due to market weakness.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are residential and commercial construction, while industrial markets remain weak.

evidence.text: Residential and Commercial new construction has seen moderate growth in the United States which is positively impacting the results of our Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) businesses. Non-residential new construction continues to grow slowly in Europe and Asia. In addition, HVAC equipment replacement and aftermarket continue to experience steady growth. However, we have seen slower worldwide industrial equipment activity, which is slightly offset by continued growth in services.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Residential and commercial construction growth offset by weak industrial markets.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable with material inflation partially offset by pricing improvements.

evidence.text: Operating margin increased from 13.6% for the three months ended September 30, 2015 to 14.1% for the same period in 2016. The increase was primarily the result of pricing improvements in excess of material inflation (0.5%) and productivity benefits in excess of other inflation (0.2%).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are stable with material inflation offset by pricing improvements.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing improvements, productivity benefits, and cost management.

evidence.text: Operating margin increased from 13.6% for the three months ended September 30, 2015 to 14.1% for the same period in 2016. The increase was primarily the result of pricing improvements in excess of material inflation (0.5%) and productivity benefits in excess of other inflation (0.2%).

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to pricing improvements, productivity benefits, and cost management.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with unallocated corporate expenses decreasing by 12.6%.

evidence.text: Unallocated corporate expense for the three months ended September 30, 2016 decreased by (12.6)% or $ 6.9 million , compared with the same period of 2015. The decrease in expenses primarily relates to reductions in professional expenses as well as the timing of general corporate expenses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from cost reduction programs indicate positive progress.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by operational excellence strategies and efficiency gains.

evidence.text: We continue to focus on operational excellence strategies as a central theme to improving our earnings and cash flows. In addition, we believe our order rates are indicative of future revenue and thus a key measure of anticipated performance.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity driven by operational excellence suggests positive trends.

factors.summary: Management conveys confidence through increased dividends and investments in innovation and new products.

evidence.text: In October 2016, we announced an increase in our quarterly share dividend from $0.32 to $0.40 per ordinary share. It reflects a 25% increase that will begin with our December 2016 payment. In February 2016, we increased our quarterly share dividend from $0.29 to $0.32 per ordinary share. In addition, we are investing substantial resources to innovate and develop new products and services which we expect will drive our future growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased dividends and investments signal management confidence.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include weak industrial markets and unfavorable foreign currency impacts.

evidence.text: Current economic conditions remain challenging across each of the segments in which we participate. Residential and Commercial new construction has seen moderate growth in the United States which is positively impacting the results of our Heating, Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) businesses. Non-residential new construction continues to grow slowly in Europe and Asia. In addition, HVAC equipment replacement and aftermarket continue to experience steady growth. However, we have seen slower worldwide industrial equipment activity, which is slightly offset by continued growth in services.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Weak industrial markets and foreign currency impacts pose risks.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on sustainable growth through innovation, geographic expansion, and operational efficiency.

evidence.text: To achieve our mission of being a world leader in creating comfortable, sustainable and efficient environments, we continue to focus on increasing our recurring revenue stream from parts, service, used equipment and rentals; and to continuously improve the efficiencies and capabilities of the products and services of our high-potential businesses. Additional emphasis is placed on expanding market coverage in terms of geography or by taking advantage of a particular vertical market or opportunity. We also continue to focus on operational excellence strategies as a central theme to improving our earnings and cash flows.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on innovation, expansion, and efficiency supports long-term growth.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign currency exchange rate movements and economic conditions in industrial markets.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily driven by higher volumes in our Climate segment. In addition, improved pricing and incremental revenues associated with acquisitions within each segment further contributed to the year-over-year increase. These amounts were partially offset by flat volumes in our Industrial segment and overall unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed signals on foreign currency and economic conditions.

evidence.text: In addition, we are investing substantial resources to innovate and develop new products and services which we expect will drive our future growth. Our growing geographic and industry diversity, coupled with our large installed product base, provides growth opportunities within our service, parts and replacement revenue streams.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and service growth indicate positive catalysts.

These amounts were partially offset by flat volumes in our Industrial segment and overall unfavorable foreign currency exchange rate movements.

evidence.text: In October 2016, we announced an increase in our quarterly share dividend from $0.32 to $0.40 per ordinary share. It reflects a 25% increase that will begin with our December 2016 payment. In February 2016, we increased our quarterly share dividend from $0.29 to $0.32 per ordinary share.

sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns are increasing with a 25% increase in quarterly dividends.

factors.summary: The sale of the Hussmann equity investment was a significant acquisition/divestiture activity, resulting in a gain of $397.8 million.

evidence.text: On December 21, 2015, we announced the sale of our remaining equity interest in the Hussmann refrigerated display case business (''Hussmann'') as part of a transaction in which Panasonic Corporation would acquire 100 percent of Hussmann's outstanding shares. The transaction was completed on April 1, 2016. We received net proceeds of $ 422.5 million , including closing settlement amounts, for our interest and recognized a gain of $ 397.8 million on the sale.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The sale of the Hussmann equity investment resulted in a significant gain of $397.8 million.
